args.query: Gusto employee cancel or edit submitted time off request

content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Request and manage time off (for employees)

  - url: https://support.gusto.com/article/999877041000000/request-and-manage-time-off-for-employees

  - source: Gusto Help Center

  - domain: support.gusto.com


  ## Content


  # Request and manage time off


  Employees can request time off from Gusto on the web or in the mobile app. On the
  web, sign in, open **Time off**, choose the relevant policy, and start a new request.
  Enter the requested dates, choose whether the request covers full or partial days,
  add an optional note, and submit it. After submission, a manager or payroll administrator
  reviews the request. Gusto sends a notification after the request is approved or
  declined, and the employee''s time-off balance is updated.


  ## Track a submitted request


  After a request is submitted, the manager or payroll administrator receives notice
  to review it. Employees receive a notification when the request is approved or declined.
  When requested dates are approaching and no decision has been received, the employee
  should follow up directly with the manager or payroll administrator.


  ## Cancel or change a request


  An existing request cannot be edited directly. The ordinary way to change a request
  is to remove the current request and create a new one.


  That cancellation route is available only while the request has not yet been processed
  through payroll. To cancel an eligible request, open **Time off**, choose the relevant
  policy when more than one exists, open the pending request, and use **Delete request**.


  A processed request is different. When any part of paid time off has already gone
  through payroll, the employee-side delete option is no longer shown. In that situation,
  the employee should contact the company''s payroll administrator so the record can
  be adjusted.


  A declined request follows a separate lifecycle. A decline cannot be reversed by
  the administrator. If the employee still wants the time off, the employee must submit
  another request.


  ## View balances and request history


  Employees can review time-off balances and request history from the **Time off**
  area. Depending on the account and policy, the page can show available hours, requested
  hours, earning rules, annual maximums, pending requests, approved requests that
  have not yet been paid, declined requests, and past time off that has already been
  paid through payroll.


  A balance can temporarily appear different because of a pending request or a recent
  payroll run. For questions about a balance, employees should contact their payroll
  administrator and identify the relevant policy and dates.


  ## Time-off activity


  The time-off tracker provides a history of balance changes. Activity can include
  accruals, policy changes, employee requests, administrator adjustments, administrator-recorded
  requests, and historical corrections. Time off used and balances can also appear
  on paystubs.

  '
metadata: {}
title: Request and manage time off (for employees)
url: https://support.gusto.com/article/999877041000000/request-and-manage-time-off-for-employees

Your PTO request is **approved and has already been processed through payroll** for **June 26–27, 2026**. Because it has gone through payroll, Gusto likely won’t let you delete or edit it yourself.

According to Gusto, you should:

1. Contact your company’s **payroll administrator or HR** as soon as possible.
2. Tell them your plans changed and provide the PTO dates.
3. Ask them to adjust or correct the processed PTO record and payroll, if appropriate.

Gusto does not allow employees to directly edit an existing request. Deleting and resubmitting is available only before the request is processed through payroll. [Gusto Help Center](https://support.gusto.com/article/999877041000000/request-and-manage-time-off-for-employees)

args.reason: Check the status of the user's submitted paid time-off request to determine the appropriate next step after a change of plans.

args.query: Gusto employee cancel or edit submitted time off request

final_answer:
Your PTO request is **approved and has already been processed through payroll** for **June 26–27, 2026**. Because it has gone through payroll, Gusto likely won’t let you delete or edit it yourself.

According to Gusto, you should:

1. Contact your company’s **payroll administrator or HR** as soon as possible.
2. Tell them your plans changed and provide the PTO dates.
3. Ask them to adjust or correct the processed PTO record and payroll, if appropriate.

Gusto does not allow employees to directly edit an existing request. Deleting and resubmitting is available only before the request is processed through payroll. [Gusto Help Center](https://support.gusto.com/article/999877041000000/request-and-manage-time-off-for-employees)

doc_id.normalized_exact: employment gusto employee time off management

text.regex:
(?isx)
^
# A. Bind the current request to the post-payroll state.
(?=
  .*?
  (?:
    (?:already\s+)?(?:processed|run|gone|went|passed|paid|reached)
    [^.!?\n]{0,120}\bpayroll\b
    |
    \bpayroll\b
    [^.!?\n]{0,120}
    (?:processed|processing|run|gone|went|passed|paid|reached)
  )
)

# B. Employee self-service edit/cancel/delete is unavailable.
(?=
  .*?
  (?:
    (?:
      cannot|can\s*not|can['’]?t|won['’]?t|will\s+not|
      unable\s+to|not\s+able\s+to|no\s+longer|
      not\s+(?:available|allowed|permitted|possible)|
      does\s+not\s+(?:allow|let)|doesn['’]?t\s+(?:allow|let)|
      no\s+(?:longer\s+)?(?:option|way|ability)\s+to
    )
    [^.!?\n]{0,180}
    (?:edit|modify|change|cancel|delete|remove|self[-_\s]*service)
    |
    (?:edit|modify|change|cancel|delete|remove|self[-_\s]*service|delete[-_\s]*option)
    [^.!?\n]{0,180}
    (?:
      cannot|can\s*not|can['’]?t|won['’]?t|will\s+not|
      unavailable|not\s+(?:available|allowed|permitted|possible)|
      no\s+longer|not\s+(?:shown|displayed)|
      isn['’]?t\s+(?:shown|displayed)
    )
  )
)

# C. Payroll administration owns the correction/adjustment.
(?=
  .*?
  (?:
    (?:
      payroll[-_\s]*(?:admin(?:istrator)?|team|department|office|staff)|
      (?:admin(?:istrator)?|team|department|office|staff)[-_\s]*(?:with[-_\s]*)?payroll|
      hr\s*[/&-]\s*payroll|payroll\s*[/&-]\s*hr
    )
    [^.!?\n]{0,200}
    (?:
      adjust(?:ment|ed|ing)?|correct(?:ion|ed|ing)?|reverse(?:d|ing)?|
      update(?:d|ing)?|fix(?:ed|ing)?|amend(?:ed|ing)?|
      reconcile(?:d|ing)?|restore(?:d|ing)?|handle(?:d|ing)?
    )
    |
    (?:
      adjust(?:ment|ed|ing)?|correct(?:ion|ed|ing)?|reverse(?:d|ing)?|
      update(?:d|ing)?|fix(?:ed|ing)?|amend(?:ed|ing)?|
      reconcile(?:d|ing)?|restore(?:d|ing)?|handle(?:d|ing)?
    )
    [^.!?\n]{0,200}
    (?:
      payroll[-_\s]*(?:admin(?:istrator)?|team|department|office|staff)|
      (?:admin(?:istrator)?|team|department|office|staff)[-_\s]*(?:with[-_\s]*)?payroll|
      hr\s*[/&-]\s*payroll|payroll\s*[/&-]\s*hr
    )
  )
)

# D. Reject an affirmative delete-and-resubmit instruction.
(?!
  .*?
  (?:you|the\s+employee|employee)\s+
  (?:
    can(?!\s*(?:not|no\s+longer|['’]?t))|
    should(?!\s+not)|must(?!\s+not)|need(?:s)?\s+to
  )
  [^.!?\n]{0,100}
  (?:delete|cancel|remove)
  [^.!?\n]{0,120}
  (?:re[-_\s]*submit|submit\s+(?:a\s+)?new|create\s+(?:a\s+)?new)
)
.*
$
